---
title: "GBR catchment area Grid-to-Grid (G2G) surface flows with eReefs GBR4 Hydro salinity (AIMS, Source: CSIRO, BOM)"
format:
  html:
    template: ../../custom_template.html
params:
  product-name: GBR catchment area Grid-to-Grid (G2G) surface flows with eReefs GBR4 Hydro salinity
---

# GBR catchment area Grid-to-Grid (G2G) surface flows with eReefs GBR4 Hydro salinity (AIMS, Source: CSIRO, BOM)

[<img src="../../images/g2g/preview-image-2019-02-06.png" alt="Preview map of GBR catchment surface flow and ocean 
salinity (2019-02-06)" style="display: block; margin: 0 auto; width: 60%; height: auto; max-width: 100%;">](https://nextcloud.eatlas.org.au/apps/sharealias/a/QLD_BOM_eReefs-g2gflow_2011-2023?path=%2Fdaily%2Fvisualisations)

<p style="text-align: center;"><em>Preview image of the full visualisation videos. Please open the downloads below to 
access the actual videos.</em></p>

[Open full visualisation downloads (MP4)](https://nextcloud.eatlas.org.au/apps/sharealias/a/QLD_BOM_eReefs-g2gflow_2011-2023?path=%2Fdaily%2Fvisualisations)

<hr/>

<div class="citation">
  <p>
    <strong>When using this visualisation, please cite:</strong> Hammerton, M., Lawrey, E. (2026). GBR catchment area 
    Grid-to-Grid (G2G) surface flows with eReefs GBR4 Hydro salinity (AIMS, Source: CSIRO, BOM). eReefs AIMS 
    Visualisation Portal. <a href="https://ereefs.aims.gov.au/gbr4_v4/surface-flow-and-ocean-salinity/">https://ereefs.aims.gov.au/gbr4_v4/surface-flow-and-ocean-salinity/</a>
  </p>
</div>

<hr/>

**Note:** Temporal coverage in this published dataset is 2007-01-01 to 2023-12-31. Modelled years 2012, 2014, 2016, 
2020, 2022, and 2023 are excluded from publication outputs due to a known export issue, where flow areas greater than 
10 cumecs were set to null values, making those files unsuitable for reuse. The original model configuration was tuned 
for river mouth flow rates, so uncertainty is higher in inland and ungauged areas.

<hr/>

This visualisation combines catchment surface flow from the Grid-to-Grid (G2G) model with marine salinity from eReefs 
GBR4 Hydro to show how land runoff events influence coastal ocean conditions across the Great Barrier Reef. During high 
rainfall and flood events, increases in land and river surface flow are associated with lowered salinity in adjacent 
marine waters, making plume pathways and regional influence patterns easier to interpret.

It is designed as an integrated land-to-sea view. The G2G layer highlights where freshwater is generated and routed 
through catchments, while the salinity layer shows the corresponding marine response.

## G2G surface flow

The `g2gflow` variable represents modelled surface freshwater flow across GBR catchments, including river flow and 
ungauged overland runoff routed toward the coast. In the published dataset this variable is provided as daily NetCDF 
grids (daily averages from hourly source data) with units of cumecs ($m^3/s$).

In this visualisation, higher `g2gflow` values identify where rainfall-driven runoff and river discharge are most 
intense, helping to show which catchment areas are contributing freshwater to marine waters during flood events. It 
should be noted that the model was tuned for generating correct river mouth flow rates, not flow rates far from the 
coast.

## Salinity

Salinity is a measure of the saltiness of seawater. Freshwater outflow from rivers and runoff lowers coastal salinity, 
so low-salinity areas are a practical indicator of flood plume influence. In this combined view, the marine `salt` 
field from eReefs GBR4 Hydro is shown alongside `g2gflow` so you can directly compare runoff intensity on land with 
decreasing salinity in adjacent coastal waters.

During high-flow periods, salinity reductions become more extensive along the coast and may spread further offshore 
depending on prevailing wind and circulation conditions. This makes the `salt` layer useful for understanding the ocean 
response to catchment runoff events and for tracking the relative footprint of plume conditions in reef-adjacent waters.


## Source data

The visualisation combines:

- Daily 1 km G2G surface flow dataset for GBR catchments (DOI: [10.26274/0K5S-5E13](https://doi.org/10.26274/0K5S-5E13)).
- eReefs GBR4 v4 daily hydrodynamic salinity from the [AIMS THREDDS server](https://thredds.ereefs.aims.gov.au/thredds/catalog/ereefs/gbr4_v4.html?dataset=gbr4_v4-daily).
